title: Dhammapada
subtitle: The Path of the Dhamma
description: A collection of sayings of the Buddha in verse form, one of the most widely read and best-known Buddhist scriptures.
summary: The Dhammapada contains 423 verses organized into 26 chapters, each dealing with a specific theme. The verses reflect core Buddhist teachings on mindfulness, ethical conduct, mental discipline, and the path to enlightenment.

verses.text: Mind is the forerunner of all actions. All deeds are led by mind, created by mind.
verses.translation: Mind is the forerunner of all actions. All deeds are led by mind, created by mind.
verses.explanation: Verse 1 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: If one speaks or acts with a corrupted mind, suffering follows, as the wheel follows the hoof of the draft-ox.
verses.translation: If one speaks or acts with a corrupted mind, suffering follows, as the wheel follows the hoof of the draft-ox.
verses.explanation: Verse 2 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: If one speaks or acts with a serene mind, happiness follows, as a shadow that never departs.
verses.translation: If one speaks or acts with a serene mind, happiness follows, as a shadow that never departs.
verses.explanation: Verse 3 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: He abused me, he struck me, he overpowered me, he robbed me. Those who harbor such thoughts do not still their hatred.
verses.translation: He abused me, he struck me, he overpowered me, he robbed me. Those who harbor such thoughts do not still their hatred.
verses.explanation: Verse 4 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: He abused me, he struck me, he overpowered me, he robbed me. Those who do not harbor such thoughts still their hatred.
verses.translation: He abused me, he struck me, he overpowered me, he robbed me. Those who do not harbor such thoughts still their hatred.
verses.explanation: Verse 5 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Hatred is never appeased by hatred in this world. By non-hatred alone is hatred appeased. This is a law eternal.
verses.translation: Hatred is never appeased by hatred in this world. By non-hatred alone is hatred appeased. This is a law eternal.
verses.explanation: Verse 6 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: There are those who do not realize that one day we all must die. Those who do realize this settle their quarrels.
verses.translation: There are those who do not realize that one day we all must die. Those who do realize this settle their quarrels.
verses.explanation: Verse 7 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: He who looks for a friend without faults will be without a friend. There is no friend without faults.
verses.translation: He who looks for a friend without faults will be without a friend. There is no friend without faults.
verses.explanation: Verse 8 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: When a wise man reproves he who is blameworthy, he is liked by the good and disliked by the bad.
verses.translation: When a wise man reproves he who is blameworthy, he is liked by the good and disliked by the bad.
verses.explanation: Verse 9 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Do not seek evil companions, do not seek the company of the vile. Seek the company of good men.
verses.translation: Do not seek evil companions, do not seek the company of the vile. Seek the company of good men.
verses.explanation: Verse 10 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: He who drinks deep the Dhamma lives happily with a tranquil mind. The wise man ever delights in the Dhamma made known by the Noble One.
verses.translation: He who drinks deep the Dhamma lives happily with a tranquil mind. The wise man ever delights in the Dhamma made known by the Noble One.
verses.explanation: Verse 11 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Irrigators regulate the waters, fletchers straighten arrow shafts, carpenters shape wood, the wise control themselves.
verses.translation: Irrigators regulate the waters, fletchers straighten arrow shafts, carpenters shape wood, the wise control themselves.
verses.explanation: Verse 12 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: As a solid rock is not shaken by the wind, even so the wise are not ruffled by praise or blame.
verses.translation: As a solid rock is not shaken by the wind, even so the wise are not ruffled by praise or blame.
verses.explanation: Verse 13 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: On hearing the Teachings, the wise become pure, like a lake that is deep, clear, and still.
verses.translation: On hearing the Teachings, the wise become pure, like a lake that is deep, clear, and still.
verses.explanation: Verse 14 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The good renounce all attachment. The good do not speak with attachment. When pleasure or pain is felt, the good are not elated or depressed.
verses.translation: The good renounce all attachment. The good do not speak with attachment. When pleasure or pain is felt, the good are not elated or depressed.
verses.explanation: Verse 15 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Not for mother's sake, not for father's sake, not for any other relative's sake is a man to do wrong.
verses.translation: Not for mother's sake, not for father's sake, not for any other relative's sake is a man to do wrong.
verses.explanation: Verse 16 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Not for mother's sake, not for father's sake, not for any other relative's sake is a man to do good.
verses.translation: Not for mother's sake, not for father's sake, not for any other relative's sake is a man to do good.
verses.explanation: Verse 17 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The good shine from afar, like the Himalaya mountain. The wicked are not seen, like arrows shot in the night.
verses.translation: The good shine from afar, like the Himalaya mountain. The wicked are not seen, like arrows shot in the night.
verses.explanation: Verse 18 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: He who sits alone, sleeps alone, walks alone, and strives to subdue himself, will find delight in the wilderness.
verses.translation: He who sits alone, sleeps alone, walks alone, and strives to subdue himself, will find delight in the wilderness.
verses.explanation: Verse 19 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Rejoice not in the harm done to others. The wise do not rejoice in injury to any being.
verses.translation: Rejoice not in the harm done to others. The wise do not rejoice in injury to any being.
verses.explanation: Verse 20 from the Yamaka Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Heedfulness is the path to immortality, heedlessness the path to death. The heedful do not die, the heedless are already dead.
verses.translation: Heedfulness is the path to immortality, heedlessness the path to death. The heedful do not die, the heedless are already dead.
verses.explanation: Verse 21 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Knowing this path well, the wise rejoice in heedfulness.
verses.translation: Knowing this path well, the wise rejoice in heedfulness.
verses.explanation: Verse 22 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The wise enter the trances and delight in the transcending of attachments.
verses.translation: The wise enter the trances and delight in the transcending of attachments.
verses.explanation: Verse 23 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: They enter the land of Dhamma, delight in the Dhamma, and find joy in the Dhamma.
verses.translation: They enter the land of Dhamma, delight in the Dhamma, and find joy in the Dhamma.
verses.explanation: Verse 24 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Let us live in joy, in the midst of hatred. Let us live in the midst of ill-will.
verses.translation: Let us live in joy, in the midst of hatred. Let us live in the midst of ill-will.
verses.explanation: Verse 25 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Let us live in joy, in the midst of disease. Let us live in the midst of grief.
verses.translation: Let us live in joy, in the midst of disease. Let us live in the midst of grief.
verses.explanation: Verse 26 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Let us live in joy, in the midst of those who are free from greed.
verses.translation: Let us live in joy, in the midst of those who are free from greed.
verses.explanation: Verse 27 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Let us live in joy, though we possess nothing. Let us live like the gods who shine with splendor.
verses.translation: Let us live in joy, though we possess nothing. Let us live like the gods who shine with splendor.
verses.explanation: Verse 28 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Victory over others is the source of enmity. Victory over oneself is the source of happiness.
verses.translation: Victory over others is the source of enmity. Victory over oneself is the source of happiness.
verses.explanation: Verse 29 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: He who has conquered himself is greater than he who has conquered a thousand times a thousand men in battle.
verses.translation: He who has conquered himself is greater than he who has conquered a thousand times a thousand men in battle.
verses.explanation: Verse 30 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The gods themselves do not honor the man who is undisciplined, lazy, and always sleeping.
verses.translation: The gods themselves do not honor the man who is undisciplined, lazy, and always sleeping.
verses.explanation: Verse 31 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: He who does not neglect the Dhamma, who is well-behaved and committed to the Dhamma, lives in joy.
verses.translation: He who does not neglect the Dhamma, who is well-behaved and committed to the Dhamma, lives in joy.
verses.explanation: Verse 32 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Let a man live in Dhamma, let him live as the Dhamma directs. The wise are ever honored in the Dhamma.
verses.translation: Let a man live in Dhamma, let him live as the Dhamma directs. The wise are ever honored in the Dhamma.
verses.explanation: Verse 33 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: As a reservoir is filled with water, even so the wise fill their minds with Dhamma.
verses.translation: As a reservoir is filled with water, even so the wise fill their minds with Dhamma.
verses.explanation: Verse 34 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: As a pitcher is filled with water, even so the wise fill their minds with Dhamma.
verses.translation: As a pitcher is filled with water, even so the wise fill their minds with Dhamma.
verses.explanation: Verse 35 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The wise shake off attachments like the wind shakes off the dust of a tree.
verses.translation: The wise shake off attachments like the wind shakes off the dust of a tree.
verses.explanation: Verse 36 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The good are like the lilies in the pond. Their fragrance travels against the wind.
verses.translation: The good are like the lilies in the pond. Their fragrance travels against the wind.
verses.explanation: Verse 37 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Of all fragrances, the fragrance of virtue rises against the wind and pervades all directions.
verses.translation: Of all fragrances, the fragrance of virtue rises against the wind and pervades all directions.
verses.explanation: Verse 38 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Of all fragrances, the fragrance of virtue rises above all other fragrances.
verses.translation: Of all fragrances, the fragrance of virtue rises above all other fragrances.
verses.explanation: Verse 39 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: Small is the fragrance that spreads in all directions. The fragrance of the good is known in all directions.
verses.translation: Small is the fragrance that spreads in all directions. The fragrance of the good is known in all directions.
verses.explanation: Verse 40 from the Appamada Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The mind is difficult to restrain, swift, and flies where it wishes. To tame the mind is good.
verses.translation: The mind is difficult to restrain, swift, and flies where it wishes. To tame the mind is good.
verses.explanation: Verse 41 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The mind is difficult to see, subtle, and flies where it wishes. The wise guard the mind.
verses.translation: The mind is difficult to see, subtle, and flies where it wishes. The wise guard the mind.
verses.explanation: Verse 42 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The mind, if wrongly directed, brings bondage. The mind, if rightly directed, brings happiness.
verses.translation: The mind, if wrongly directed, brings bondage. The mind, if rightly directed, brings happiness.
verses.explanation: Verse 43 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: If one holds the mind unsteady, as a charioteer holds unbridled horses, the passions will be removed.
verses.translation: If one holds the mind unsteady, as a charioteer holds unbridled horses, the passions will be removed.
verses.explanation: Verse 44 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The mind is very strong. It is swift and difficult to catch. If the mind is well-controlled, it brings happiness.
verses.translation: The mind is very strong. It is swift and difficult to catch. If the mind is well-controlled, it brings happiness.
verses.explanation: Verse 45 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The mind is like a fish moving in water. It cannot be caught by any other than the wise.
verses.translation: The mind is like a fish moving in water. It cannot be caught by any other than the wise.
verses.explanation: Verse 46 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The mind is like a bird flying in the air. It cannot be caught by any other than the wise.
verses.translation: The mind is like a bird flying in the air. It cannot be caught by any other than the wise.
verses.explanation: Verse 47 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The mind is like a bird flying in the sky. It cannot be caught by any other than the wise.
verses.translation: The mind is like a bird flying in the sky. It cannot be caught by any other than the wise.
verses.explanation: Verse 48 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The mind is like a tree, covered with leaves and flowers. If the mind is well-controlled, it brings happiness.
verses.translation: The mind is like a tree, covered with leaves and flowers. If the mind is well-controlled, it brings happiness.
verses.explanation: Verse 49 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.

verses.text: The mind is difficult to see, subtle, and flies where it wishes. If the mind is well-controlled, it brings happiness.
verses.translation: The mind is difficult to see, subtle, and flies where it wishes. If the mind is well-controlled, it brings happiness.
verses.explanation: Verse 50 from the Citta Vagga chapter of the Dhammapada.
